title: Moldova Entry Visa Application (Cerere de solicitare a vizei de intrare în Republica Moldova / Application for an Entry Visa to the Republic of Moldova)
description: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and European Integration's (MFAEI) standard consular application form for a national entry visa to the Republic of Moldova, submitted by third-country nationals at a Moldovan embassy or consulate abroad. The 33-item layout, printed declaration text, and required supporting documents closely mirror the EU-harmonised uniform ('Schengen', Annex I to Regulation (EC) No 810/2009) short-stay visa application template — Moldova is not a Schengen or EU member state, but its own national visa form reuses the same field structure and much of the same printed wording, substituting 'Republic of Moldova' for 'Schengen area'/'Member State(s)' throughout and omitting the EU template's multi-country fields (destination Member State(s), Member State of first entry) since this is a single-country visa. This document models the applicant-facing content of MFAEI's own central, unauthenticated, English-language 3-page specimen (corroborated during scouting by an identical mirror hosted by the Moldovan consulate in Iași, Romania): applicant identity, travel document, contact, occupation, purpose-of-journey, prior-visa/biometric history, host/inviting-party, and travel-funding data, plus a family-member block for applicants related to a Moldovan citizen. GovSchema is an independent, non-profit standards body and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of the Republic of Moldova, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and European Integration, or any Moldovan embassy or consulate.

authority.name: Ministerul Afacerilor Externe și Integrării Europene (Ministry of Foreign Affairs and European Integration), acting through Moldova's embassies and consulates abroad

source.documentRef: 'Application for an Entry Visa to the Republic of Moldova', MFAEI's own central English-language 3-page flat/print PDF specimen, hosted directly on mfa.gov.md

verification.notes:
GOV-3181, a disclosed backlog candidate delegated from the GOV-3157/GOV-3152 'GovSchema Standard Research' cycle. Re-verified fresh rather than trusted from the prior scouting note: HTTP 200, content-type application/pdf, 53,469 bytes, sha256 380ba14e0eeb2a005a71783261a7a12c4ff01b4899fbdd4e7e499f2d543d2c03, genuine %PDF-1.5 header, no login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate on mfa.gov.md. Extracted with pdfjs-dist@3.11.174: 3 pages, a genuine native text layer (277/132/29 text items per page), zero AcroForm widgets on any page (getAnnotations() returns 0 throughout) — a flat, non-fillable print-and-fill specimen, consistent with this registry's established convention for such forms. Every field, checkbox label, and option set below was read directly from the coordinate-sorted text extraction (y/x position of each text run), cross-checked against a node-canvas box-grid render of pages 2-3 at 2.5x scale to confirm row/column structure where glyph rendering itself was unavailable in this sandbox (missing standard font data); no field numbering or wording was assumed from prior knowledge of the cognate EU Annex I template without confirming it against this extraction.

**Not a duplicate of this registry's existing EU-Annex-I-style visa siblings** (at/bmeia/schengen-visa-application, gr/mfa/application-for-schengen-visa, es/maec/solicitud-visado-nacional, il/mfa/entry-visa-application, among others): while the field structure and much of the printed wording is near-identical to the EU-harmonised template (both this form and the EU Annex I form share the same 1-31 applicant-data field sequence, the same sex/marital-status/travel-document-type/purpose-of-journey/number-of-entries enumerations, and the same two-sided applicant/sponsor travel-funding block), this Moldovan national form differs substantively: (1) it is a single-country visa with no 'Member State(s) of destination' or 'Member State of first entry' fields, unlike every Schengen-area sibling; (2) its prior-visa lookback window is 12 months (not 3 years) and is framed entirely around 'the Republic of Moldova' rather than 'the Schengen area'; (3) it carries no asterisked EU/EEA/CH-family-member exemption footnote anywhere (confirmed absent by a full-text scan for '*') — unlike at/bmeia, this specimen's family-member section (item 31) is instead framed around relatives who are themselves 'citizen[s] of the Republic of Moldova', a genuinely different eligibility population, not a cosmetic relabelling; (4) its closing declaration names Moldova's own 'SIA \"Consul\"' data system (not the EU's VIS) and the Ministry of the Interior Affairs of the Republic of Moldova as the responsible data authority; and (5) uniquely among this registry's EU-Annex-I-style siblings reviewed so far, it carries **two separate, structurally distinct place/date-plus-signature blocks** rather than one (see the next paragraph) — confirmed a genuine two-step sign-off, not a duplicate-content-stream rendering artifact, since the surrounding text differs completely between the two occurrences and a node-canvas render of both pages confirms two visually distinct single-row boxes at the expected coordinates.

**Two signature blocks, disclosed and both modelled.** The form's own numbered item 32 ('Place and date') and item 33 ('Signature (for minors, signature of parental authority/legal guardian)') form the final row of the main tabular data-entry section, immediately after the item-31 family-member block, with no declarative text of its own printed alongside it — modelled as signaturePlace/signatureDate, paired with an applicationSignature documents[] attestation. Independently, after several paragraphs of closing declaration text spanning the remainder of page 2 and all of page 3 (visa-fee non-refundability; the travel-medical-insurance requirement for multiple-entry applicants; consent to data/photograph/fingerprint collection and processing under the SIA 'Consul' system for up to five years; a data-subject-rights notice; an accuracy-of-particulars declaration; and an undertaking to leave Moldovan territory before visa expiry), the source prints a second, unnumbered 'Place and date' / 'Signature (for minors, signature of parental authority/legal guardian)' row — modelled as declarationPlace/declarationDate, paired with a closingDeclaration documents[] attestation carrying a paraphrase (not a verbatim quote, per this registry's established convention, e.g. at/bmeia/schengen-visa-application) of that full closing text.

**Requiredness judgment calls** (the source carries no asterisk, bolding, or other printed required/optional convention anywhere on any of its 3 pages, confirmed by a full-text scan for '*'): core identity, travel-document, contact-address/phone, occupation, employer, purpose-of-journey, entries-requested, duration, prior-visa/fingerprint-history, arrival/departure-date, host, and both signature-block fields are modelled required; minors'-guardian details, national identity number (itself printed 'where applicable'), applicant e-mail address, inviting-party telephone/telefax, inviting-company block, all travel-funding checkboxes/means-of-support checkboxes, and the entire item-31 Moldovan-relative block are modelled optional, since each applies only to a subset of applicants (minors, dual/registered nationals, business-hosted travellers, a specific funding arrangement, or a Moldovan-citizen family tie) rather than to every applicant. Unlike at/bmeia/schengen-visa-application's asterisked items 19/20/31/32/33 (occupation, employer, host, inviting-company, and funding — exempted there only for EU/EEA/CH family members under a printed footnote this Moldovan specimen does not carry), currentOccupation and employerOrEducationalEstablishmentDetails are modelled required here, since no equivalent exemption is printed on this form.

The office-processing box on page 1 ('For official use only': date of application, visa application number, lodging channel, file handler, a supporting-documents-received checklist, the visa decision itself, category A/C/LTV, validity dates, and a second, office-facing 'number of entries'/'number of days' pair distinct from the applicant-facing items 22/23) is entirely consular-staff-completed data, not applicant-supplied, and is out of scope — the same convention this registry applies to other jurisdictions' clerk-only intake/decision sections (e.g. il/mfa/entry-visa-application's 'for office use' box). The printed '(x)' marker on items 1-3 (surname, surname at birth, first names) carries no further explanatory footnote text recoverable anywhere in this 3-page specimen (unlike at/bmeia/schengen-visa-application's fuller EU template, which explains it elsewhere); disclosed here as an unresolved marker rather than assumed to mean the same 'per travel document' instruction the cognate EU form states explicitly, though that is the plausible reading given the shared template lineage.

2 valid mock scenarios (a self-funded tourist applying alone, with no prior Moldovan visa or fingerprints on file; a business traveller invited by a Moldovan company, with a spouse recorded as a Moldovan-citizen family member and their own fingerprints previously collected) plus 7 mutation-control fixtures (a missing statically-required field, a missing conditionally-required field each for the marital-status/travel-document/purpose-of-journey "other" branches, a missing signature-block field, an invalid enum value, and an invalid date format) are committed under conformance/md/mfa/entry-visa-application/1.0.0/. An ephemeral, from-scratch conformance checker (deriving required/requiredWhen rules directly from this schema's own fields[]/documents[], discarded after use, not committed) ran all 9: both valid scenarios at 0 errors, all 7 mutation controls each raising exactly 1 error. Validated clean with node tools/validate.mjs and node tools/validate-ajv.mjs, individually and as part of the full registry run. Opens Moldova's Visa vertical (2nd of 6, after DMV via md/asp/vehicle-registration, GOV-3157); Taxes remains the last open, previously-scouted vertical for Moldova, and Passport/National ID remain confirmed dead ends from the GOV-3152 cycle.

fields.name: declarationPlace
fields.label: Place of signature (closing declaration)
fields.description: A second, unnumbered 'Place and date' field printed at the very end of page 3, after the full closing-declaration text — structurally distinct from signaturePlace, which closes the main data-entry section. See this document's verification notes for the disclosure of this two-signature structure.
fields.sourceRef: 'Place and date' (unnumbered, foot of page 3, after the closing declaration text)

fields.label: Date of signature (closing declaration)
fields.description: See declarationPlace for the two-signature-structure disclosure.
fields.sourceRef: 'Place and date' (unnumbered, foot of page 3, after the closing declaration text)

documents.label: Applicant's photograph
documents.sourceRef: 'PHOTO' placeholder box, top right of page 1

documents.label: Signature confirming the application data (items 1-31), or (for minors) of the parental authority/legal guardian
documents.statement: The applicant's (or, for minors, their parental authority/legal guardian's) signature at item 33, closing the main data-entry section (items 1-31). No declarative text is printed alongside item 32/33 itself on the source; the substance being attested to is simply the accuracy of the preceding data fields.
documents.sourceRef: '33. Signature (for minors, signature of parental authority/legal guardian)', paired with signaturePlace/signatureDate (item 32)

documents.label: Signature confirming the closing declaration, or (for minors) of the parental authority/legal guardian
documents.statement: Paraphrased from the source form's closing declaration text (not a verbatim quote — see this document's verification notes): the applicant is aware the visa fee is not refunded if the application is refused; is aware of the need for adequate travel medical insurance for the first stay and any subsequent visits, where a multiple-entry visa is applied for; consents to the collection of the data on this form, a photograph, and (where applicable) fingerprints, and to their supply to and processing by the relevant authorities of the Republic of Moldova for the purpose of a visa decision; is aware that this data, and any decision on the application (or on annulling, revoking, or extending an issued visa), will be entered into and stored in the SIA 'Consul' system for up to five years, accessible to Moldovan visa, border-check, immigration, and asylum authorities, and (under certain conditions) to authorities responsible for preventing and investigating terrorist and other serious criminal offences, with the Ministry of the Interior Affairs of the Republic of Moldova as the responsible processing authority; is aware of the right to obtain notification of, and request correction or deletion of, their own recorded data, and that the national supervisory authority hears related claims; declares that, to the best of their knowledge, all particulars supplied are correct and complete, and is aware that false statements may lead to refusal or annulment of a visa and to prosecution; and undertakes to leave the territory of the Republic of Moldova before the expiry of a granted visa, acknowledging that possession of a visa is only one prerequisite for entry, does not entitle the holder to compensation if entry is refused, and that entry prerequisites are checked again at the border.
documents.sourceRef: Unnumbered closing declaration text spanning the remainder of page 2 and all of page 3, paired with declarationPlace/declarationDate
